categories.label: Lead Capture And Conversion
categories.business_value: Turns traffic into estimate requests, calls, and booked-job opportunities.
categories.why_add: Most contractor sites leak demand after the user arrives. This category forces every page to answer what service is offered, where it is offered, why the contractor is credible, and how to contact them immediately.
categories.evidence_summary: Google Ads distinguishes lead-form volume from higher-intent lead quality. GA4 can measure lead-generation form submissions as key events, so conversion should be designed and tracked as a first-class product surface.
product_modules: thank-you page and offline conversion handoff
automations: detect pages without a primary CTA
test tel links and form endpoints
route high-intent services to priority follow-up
compliance_risks: asking for insurance or damage claims beyond approved intake
mvp_actions: Create standard roofing estimate form fields.
Mark form submit as generate_lead/key event.
quality_tests: CTA exists on mobile and desktop.
Form works with required fields only.
Lead event fires once per submit.
Privacy policy is linked before any paid lead form.

sources.label: Google Ads - Use lead forms in campaigns
sources.use_for: Lead form volume versus qualification tradeoffs in paid campaigns.

sources.use_for: Lead form privacy policy, advertiser eligibility, sensitive vertical, and data-use requirements.

sources.use_for: Marking important business actions as key events and reporting on cross-channel performance.

sources.label: Google Analytics - Measure lead-generation form submissions as key events
sources.use_for: Lead form event measurement and key event setup.

categories.label: Tracking, Attribution, And ROI Loop
categories.business_value: Proves whether the product is creating real pipeline, not just impressions or pretty pages.
categories.why_add: Frame needs to report leads, qualified leads, booked jobs, and revenue assumptions by source, page, city, and service. This is the difference between a build project and a monthly growth system.
categories.evidence_summary: Search Console provides page/query click and impression data. GBP performance reports expose actions such as calls, website clicks, and directions. GA4 key events can mark important business actions and attribute them across channels.
automations: ingest GBP calls and website clicks
flag pages with impressions but low CTR
compliance_risks: combining personal lead data without access controls
mvp_actions: Build a monthly booked-job feedback form.
quality_tests: Every lead has source and landing page.
Every report separates leads from booked jobs.
GSC data is filtered by query/page.
GBP metrics are not treated as exact call totals.

sources.use_for: Automated Search Console query/page/country/device trend extraction.

categories.label: Google Business Profile Operations
categories.business_value: Improves local trust, map-pack eligibility, customer action visibility, and monthly operations.
categories.why_add: For roofers, GBP is often the highest-intent local discovery surface. The product needs a profile-change queue, photo/update cadence, review response workflow, and profile performance digest.
categories.evidence_summary: Google says local results are mainly based on relevance, distance, and prominence, and recommends complete accurate info, verification, updated hours, review replies, and photos/videos. GBP posts can share updates, offers, and events, but they are reviewed against policy.
automations: draft service updates from approved sitemap
suggest photo uploads from completed jobs
quality_tests: No GBP edit is published without owner approval.

categories.label: Reviews And Reputation Engine
categories.business_value: Compounds trust, improves conversion, supports local prominence, and creates proof language for pages.
categories.why_add: Roofing decisions are trust-heavy. Reviews should become a compliant operating loop: request, monitor, respond, categorize by service/city, and reuse approved themes without fabricating testimonials.
categories.evidence_summary: Google permits merchants to ask for genuine-experience reviews without incentives, but bans fake engagement, review gating, incentives, and competitor manipulation. The FTC rule adds federal risk around fake reviews, deceptive testimonials, suppression, and insider reviews. Yelp is stricter and says not to ask for Yelp reviews.
quality_tests: Every testimonial reuse has source and approval.
Negative reviews get response drafts, not suppression tactics.

sources.use_for: Review, photo, Maps contribution, fake engagement, incentives, review gating, impersonation, and personal-information boundaries.

categories.label: Backlink And Citation Acquisition
categories.business_value: Builds local prominence and referral trust without exposing clients to link-spam risk.
categories.why_add: For Dallas roofers, the best backlinks are not generic SEO links. They are real local and trade proof: manufacturer profiles, supplier pages, chambers, sponsorships, local press, project partners, industry directories, vendor certifications, and citation consistency.
categories.evidence_summary: Google's local ranking guidance names prominence and links across the web as signals, but Search spam policies prohibit links created primarily to manipulate rankings. The product must separate legitimate earned/citation opportunities from risky paid/link-scheme tactics.
automations: match vendors and manufacturers to profile pages

categories.label: City And Service Expansion Strategy
categories.business_value: Lets the contractor expand into target cities and services with useful pages instead of thin doorway pages.
categories.why_add: A Dallas roofer needs separate strategy for Dallas, Plano, Frisco, McKinney, Garland, Irving, Fort Worth, and service lines such as roof repair, storm damage, metal roofing, gutters, and inspections. The product must prevent mass-generated weak pages.
categories.evidence_summary: Google's useful-content guidance rewards pages built for people and warns against content made primarily to manipulate rankings. Local relevance also depends on complete business info and real prominence signals, not just city keyword repetition.
automations: block pages without proof or usefulness
find gaps in reviews/photos by city/service
quality_tests: Each page has unique local usefulness.
Each page has approved service area.
Each page has visible proof or a proof request.
Internal links connect related service/city pages.

sources.use_for: Content usefulness, reliability, first-hand proof, and avoiding content made primarily to manipulate search.

categories.label: Case Studies And Proof Asset System
categories.business_value: Turns completed jobs, photos, reviews, and owner expertise into conversion proof and local authority.
categories.why_add: Contractor buyers need to see evidence before they call. Proof assets also feed service pages, city pages, GBP photos, review themes, proposals, and sales demos.
categories.evidence_summary: Google recommends helpful, reliable, people-first content and complete business profiles with photos/videos. LocalBusiness schema can represent business identity, while visible page content should carry the actual proof.
automations: draft project story from approved facts
suggest proof blocks for related pages
mvp_actions: Map proof assets to city/service pages.
quality_tests: Proof is reused on relevant pages.

categories.label: AEO And AI Search Visibility
categories.business_value: Makes contractor expertise easier for AI search, AI Overviews, ChatGPT search, and answer engines to understand and cite.
categories.why_add: AEO should not be a separate magic trick. It is answer-ready SEO: crawlable pages, clear entities, direct answers, schema, citations, proof, and robots/indexing choices.
categories.evidence_summary: Google gives site-owner guidance for AI Overviews and AI Mode. OpenAI documents OAI-SearchBot access and referral tracking. Bing's IndexNow supports faster discovery for participating engines.
automations: generate answer blocks from approved content
mvp_actions: Add direct answer blocks to service pages.
Track AI visibility manually before automating claims.
quality_tests: Every answer cites approved visible content.

categories.label: Content Operations
categories.business_value: Creates a monthly growth cadence that compounds service authority, seasonal relevance, and proof reuse.
categories.why_add: The product needs to move from one-time page generation to a controlled publishing calendar: storm season, roof types, repair guides, city updates, maintenance, financing, gutters, and insurance-safe education.
categories.evidence_summary: Google's helpful-content guidance makes usefulness and reliability the baseline. Search Console query/page data should feed content iteration rather than topic guessing.
mvp_actions: Tie every topic to service/city/business proof.
Use GSC data in monthly reports.

categories.label: Competitor And SERP Intelligence
categories.business_value: Shows where the client can realistically win and which pages, proof, reviews, and authority signals are missing.
categories.why_add: Frame should not blindly generate pages. It should compare the target city/service SERP, map-pack competitors, review counts, page quality, proof depth, backlinks/citations, schema, and lead paths.
categories.evidence_summary: Search Console identifies query/page opportunities for the client's existing presence. GBP performance shows how customers interact with the profile. Google local ranking guidance frames local visibility around relevance, distance, and prominence.
owner_gates: competitor list approval when used publicly
quality_tests: Manual observations are labeled as observed.

categories.label: Sales Enablement And Proposal Assets
categories.business_value: Helps sell the product by translating technical work into buyer-visible business outcomes.
categories.why_add: Contractor owners do not buy schema, AI agents, or topic maps. They buy more qualified calls, better trust, better proof, less manual follow-up, and a clearer monthly plan.
categories.evidence_summary: The category is an application layer over sourced operating categories: local ranking, reviews, proof, tracking, and conversion. It should use conservative language and avoid ranking or lead guarantees.
automations: turn audit findings into proposal blocks
create demo script from build artifacts
mvp_actions: Connect proof assets to sales deck.
quality_tests: Every claim maps to an artifact or source.

categories.label: Client Intake And Approval Workflow
categories.business_value: Prevents bad public claims and reduces delivery drag by collecting missing facts, proof, access, and approvals early.
categories.why_add: Most contractor automation fails because the system invents or assumes facts. Intake and approval gates let agents move fast without publishing unapproved service areas, licenses, warranties, photos, reviews, or tracking changes.
categories.evidence_summary: The highest-risk categories all require owner approval: GBP edits, review requests/replies, lead forms/privacy, schema facts, public claims, proof assets, ads, and tracking. This category makes those gates reusable.
mvp_actions: Map missing facts to digest questions.
quality_tests: Every public claim has source and approval.
No account edit without access approval.
Approval status is visible to agents.

categories.label: CRM And Follow-Up Automation
categories.business_value: Reduces lead leakage after the form or call by turning inquiries into tracked follow-up and booked-job outcomes.
categories.why_add: Organic growth is wasted if leads are not contacted quickly, qualified, and marked won/lost. This category closes the loop between marketing, sales, and monthly reporting.
categories.evidence_summary: GA4 recommended events include generated, converted, unconverted, and disqualified lead states. That event model should be mirrored in CRM status fields and monthly reporting.
automations: sync booked job to ROI report

categories.label: Paid Search And LSA Bridge
categories.business_value: Lets the organic system support paid acquisition without making ads the core product.
categories.why_add: Roofing campaigns often use Google Ads, LSAs, and retargeting. Frame should be ready to support them with landing pages, tracking, privacy, GBP alignment, and lead quality feedback.
categories.evidence_summary: Google Local Services advertisers may need screening, licenses, insurance, and background checks. Google lead forms require privacy policies and policy compliance and include a volume-versus-qualified lead tradeoff.
automations: map LSA service categories to GBP/site
feed qualified leads back into reports
mvp_actions: Map paid pages to tracking events.

categories.label: Compliance And Risk Controls
categories.business_value: Keeps automation commercially usable by preventing avoidable account, legal, and trust damage.
categories.why_add: The faster the agents move, the more important the brakes become. This category protects GBP, reviews, ads, public claims, privacy, outreach, photos, tracking, and publishing.
categories.evidence_summary: Google, FTC, Yelp, Google Ads, and Local Services all carry explicit policy boundaries. The product needs reusable blocks that prevent fake engagement, unsupported claims, review manipulation, lead-form privacy failures, and risky link practices.
mvp_actions: Add approval gate per risky category.
Make blocked actions visible in reports.
quality_tests: No missing privacy policy for lead forms.

categories.label: Technical SEO And Schema Infrastructure
categories.business_value: Gives every page a crawlable, indexable, internally linked, structured foundation that supports SEO, AEO, and conversion.
categories.why_add: Without technical foundations, content and backlinks underperform. The product should ship clean metadata, crawlable navigation, schema tied to visible facts, sitemap/robots, fast mobile UI, and internal links.
categories.evidence_summary: Google's SEO starter guide and link best practices emphasize crawlable pages and links. LocalBusiness schema and Google local business structured data provide the entity layer, but schema must represent true visible information.
automations: generate LocalBusiness/Service/FAQ schema from approved facts
compliance_risks: schema claims not visible on page

categories.label: Community Platforms And Local Social
categories.business_value: Adds neighborhood trust and referral surfaces without spamming communities.
categories.why_add: Roofers can earn visibility on Nextdoor, local Facebook groups, chambers, sponsorship pages, and neighborhood references. The product should treat this as trust and referral distribution, not automated spam.
categories.evidence_summary: Nextdoor supports local business pages and business posts, but its ad policy requires accurate representation, clear advertiser identity, and privacy policy support for lead generation.
mvp_actions: Add local social post approval gate.
quality_tests: Lead capture links have privacy policy.
